reviews.review_msg: Catherine is by far my favourite teacher at SFU! She's fun, she'll joke with you and she really helps you practice your french. She's kind of like the "Fun Aunt". She is frank, which in some teachers may be intimidating, but my experiences with her are all good and i take her advice to heart. A+
She is a good, dedicated teacher whi is passionate about her profession and life in general. She is very very understanding and kind, just stay on her good side. She is great!!
She's pretty easy and nice but kind of annoying to listen to.

Giuliano was such a pleasure to have as a professor. He's sweet, kind and accepting of everyone's opinions during discussions. I can't comment on his criteria and grading as it was obvious that Giuliano had to follow a certain curriculum considering he's a new professor and expected to do so. Not his fault. All in all, un prof chill.
The sweetest guy! Just a tough marker. Would def take again
ANAS IS SO SWEET!!! Genuinely a very kind, caring and compassionate man. Would 110% take again.

My GOAT ! Grading criteria is very clear + he grades generously. He truly sets you up for success and wants to support your learning. He gives you in class time to complete assignments, so almost no homework ! I love him so much I go to his office hours just to yap. Participation + talking to him in class = easy 10%. BEST PROF EVER

One of the best profs I've ever had at SFU. Not only if he smart and very knowledgeable, but he's also genuinely one of the kindest people you will ever meet. He is friendly, accommodating, and always more than willing to lend a helping hand. This class was brilliant and I will miss it a lot!

Anas is a very kind prof and I really enjoyed his class. He is a generous grader, very approachable and is available for extra help. Sometimes his class felt a little long, but it is an easy class.

Every assignment in FREN300 is a group project. So get ready to talk to your classmates and participate in class discussions. 
Lots of presentations.

Anas was such an amazing professor. He was extremely accommadating, passionate, and very kind, perfect for an oral speaking class that requires everyone to speak in front of each other. He shared a lot about African culture, which I was not personally familiar with, but it was super cool to learn about. Il répète très souvent  « la vie est belle ».

She was a good professor in that she covered the materials in class but a terrible prof in general.  She picks favorites and she is biased.  SFU should do some background check on their "guest" professors and check up on them to see if they're doing a good job! If there are more profs like her, I am definitely transferring to a different university

Such a gem especially in the French Dept at SFU. Clear, understanding, knowledgeable, approachable, NOT overbearing and teaches really well. I have learned TONS from her and she makes it fun! Amazing assignments -- you get to do a slam poem and have a radio talk show broadcasted among other things. Your French will improve.

Extremely unreasonable prof. She assigns enough work for three courses, and gives unreasonable and unrealistic amounts of time to get everything done. The work itself is not overly difficult, but she just assigns erroneous amounts of it. It's as if she thinks this is only class you're taking. If you have a job and/or other homework, good luck.
AMAZING... haven't even finished the class yet, but I wanted to post about how passionate and interesting and fabulous Linda is... take her class, she is the best french prof I have ever had!
